revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Iizuna-jinja]][[ja:飯縄神社]] {{Expand Japanese|飯縄神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Iizuna Shrine (Q17238950) | native_name = 飯縄神社 | image = 飯縄神社 (Iizuna Zinja).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iizuna Shrine (Q17238950) | coordinates = {{coord|36.680788|138.168564}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|lt=Ōtonoji and Ōtonobe|ja|オオトノヂ・オオトノベ|zh|大戶之道尊與大苫邊尊|qid=Q11073597|1=Otonoji and Otonobe}} | website = {{Official website|http://www.iizuna-jinnjya.jp/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Iizuna Shrine'''|飯縄神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Shinano Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * district|lt=Minochi district|ja|水内郡|zh|水內郡|qid=Q11548296|1=Minochi district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.680788|138.168564}} == official website (P856) == * tiếng Nhật|vo|Yapänapük|war|Hinapon|wa|Djaponès (lingaedje)|functions|Z1830|wuu|日本闲话|xal|Нипонмудн келн|xmf|იაპონური ნინა|yi|יאפאניש|yo|Èdè Japaní|za|Vah Nyiedbonj|zgh|ⵜⵓⵜⵍⴰⵢⵜ ⵜⴰⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏⵜ|zh_classical|日語|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún-gí|zh_yue|日文|zh|日语|zhbooks|日语|zhvoyage|日语会话手册|zu|IsiJapani|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * 飯縄神社 (Iizuna Zinja).jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q11073597|ltq=Q11073597|qid=Q11073597|1=Otonoji and Otonobe}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|lt=Ukemochi|bcl|Ukemochi|ca|Uke Mochi|da|Ogetsu-no-hime|en|Ukemochi|eo|Uke Moĉi|es|Uke Mochi|eu|Ukemochi|fa|اوکه موچی|hr|Uke Mochi|it|Uke-Mochi|ja|保食神|mni|ꯎꯀꯦꯃꯣꯆꯤ|pl|Ukemochi|pt|Ukemochi|ru|Укэмоти-но ками|sl|Uke-moči|sv|Uke-Mochi|uk|Укемоті|zh|保食神|qid=Q2280323|1=Ukemochi}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181995 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * シラタマタリホノ- == Facebook username (P2013) == * iizunajinnjya == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7100005000417 == Commons category (P373) == * Iizuna-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039931|ltq=Q135039931|qid=Q135039931|1=Shiratamatarihono- Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Iizuna Shrine okumiya|lt=Iizuna Shrine okumiya|qid=Q135194234|1=Iizuna Shrine okumiya}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県長野市富田380', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Q11666652|ltq=Q11666652|qid=Q11666652|1=Mount Iizuna}} {{commons category|Category:Iizuna-jinja}} [[JA:飯縄神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17238950}} == Japanese content == {{redirect|Sumetaruhonomikoto Shrine|the shrine located in Shinshū-Shinmachi, Nagano City|Sumetaruhonomikoto Shrine (Shinshū-Shinmachi)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Iizuna Shrine |Image = [[File:Iizuna Shrine (Iizuna Jinja).jpg|270px|Satomiya worship hall]]
Satomiya worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|qid=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}} Tomita 380 |Coordinates = Okusha: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|44|10.9|N|138|7|51.3|E|display=inline}}
Satomiya: {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|40|50.9|N|138|10|7|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ōtoji no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtoji no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ōtohe no Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtohe no Mikoto}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukemochi no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} Eve Festival {{ill|lt=October 7|Q2926|qid=Q2926|1=October 7}} evening (Satomiya)
Annual Festival {{ill|lt=October 8|Q2924|qid=Q2924|1=October 8}} (Satomiya) |Rituals = {{ill|lt=Kinensai|Q118906710|qid=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} {{ill|lt=May 8|Q2555|qid=Q2555|1=May 8}} (Satomiya)
{{ill|lt=Mount Iizuna|Q11666652|qid=Q11666652|1=Mount Iizuna}} {{ill|lt=Mountain Opening Festival|Q11655214|qid=Q11655214|1=Mountain Opening Festival}} {{ill|lt=June 5|Q2622|qid=Q2622|1=June 5}} (First Torii / Okusha)
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Great Purification Ceremony|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=December 8|Q2301|qid=Q2301|1=December 8}} (Satomiya) |Map = {{Location map+|Japan Nagano|width=210|float=center|caption=|places= {{Location map~|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=44|lat_sec=10.9|lon_deg=138|lon_min=7|lon_sec=51.3|position=top|label=Okusha|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{Location map~|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=40|lat_sec=50.9|lon_deg=138|lon_min=10|lon_sec=7|position=bottom|label=Satomiya|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}}} {{OSM Location map|coord={{coord|36.708368|N|138.149745|E}}|fullscreen-option=1|zoom=11|float=center|width=200|caption=|title=|mark-coord1={{coord|36|44|10.9|N|138|7|51.3|E}}|label1=Okusha|label-pos1=top|mark-size1=8|mark-coord2={{coord|36|40|50.9|N|138|10|7|E}}|label2=Satomiya|label-pos2=top|mark-size2=8}} }} '''Iizuna Shrine''' (いいづなじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tomita, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|qid=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=main shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (sōsha) of the Iizuna Shrines enshrined throughout Japan.[http://www.iizuna-jinnjya.jp/goaisatu.htm Iizuna Shrine Official Website] Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=shrine ranks|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. === History === * Around the year 270, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtoji no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} was enshrined on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Iizuna|Q11666652|qid=Q11666652|1=Mount Iizuna}} as a {{ill|lt=Tenjin|Q1305727|qid=Q1305727|1=Tenjin}}, and the deity was called {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iizuna Daimyōjin|1=UNKNOWN}}.[http://www.iizuna-jinnjya.jp/yuisyo.htm Iizuna Shrine: Origins] * The honji (original Buddhist form) was identified as {{ill|lt=Dainichi Nyorai|Q239847|qid=Q239847|1=Vairocana}}. In {{ill|lt=848|Q30380|qid=Q30380|1=848}} (the first year of {{ill|lt=Kashō|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}}), an ascetic scholar entered Mount Iizuna in {{ill|lt=March|Q110|qid=Q110|1=March}} and beheld the revered form of the Buddha. * In {{ill|lt=1233|Q5421|qid=Q5421|1=1233}} (the first year of {{ill|lt=Tenpuku (Japan)|Q1194452|qid=Q1194452|lt=Tenpuku|1=Tenpuku}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Itō Hyōbu Dayū Buzen no Kami Tadanetsuna|1=UNKNOWN}}, the {{ill|lt=jitō|Q1329805|qid=Q1329805|1=Jitō}} of Ogino in {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|qid=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} ({{ill|lt=Shinshū-Shinmachi|Q1042382|qid=Q1042382|1=Shinshūshinmachi}}), entered the mountain following a divine revelation from Iizuna Daimyōjin and invited {{ill|lt=Iizuna Gongen|Q11666654|qid=Q11666654|lt=Iizuna Daigongen|1=Izuna Gongen}} to be enshrined on the summit. Tadanetsuna’s son, Moritsuna, also entered the mountain with him, mastered the rites of {{ill|lt=Dakiniten|Q1127224|qid=Q1127224|1=Dakini}}, inherited from his father the Iizuna ritesA unique form of esoteric practice using {{ill|lt=kuda-gitsune|Q4451648|qid=Q4451648|1=Kuda-gitsune}}, established the original Iizuna {{ill|lt=ninjutsu|Q3342041|qid=Q3342041|1=ninpō}}, styled himself “Sennichi Dayū,” spread {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iizuna belief|1=UNKNOWN}} throughout the country, and became regarded as the progenitor of ninjutsu. * In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 6), the title '''Sumetaruhonomikoto Shrine''' (すめたるほのみことじんじゃ) was bestowed by the {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefectural Government|Q22124742|qid=Q22124742|1=Nagano prefectural government}} in {{ill|lt=May|Q119|qid=Q119|1=May}}. === Genealogy of the Nishina priestly family === Below is the genealogy of the Nishina clan, who served for generations as priests of Iizuna Shrine.Shinano Digital Commons, “(Minochi District) Nishina Clan Genealogy (copy) [https://www.ro-da.jp/shinshu-dcommons/museum_history/03OD0621101700]” However, their claim to be descendants of {{ill|lt=Taira no Koremori|Q3055506|qid=Q3055506|1=Taira no Koremori}} is merely self-proclaimed. In reality, it is thought to divide into the line of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nishina Morihiro|1=UNKNOWN}} – {{ill|lt=Nishina Moriiye|Q76970446|qid=Q76970446|1=Nishina Moriiye}} – {{ill|lt=Nishina Moritō|Q11376460|qid=Q11376460|1=Nishina Moritō}} – {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nishina Morikatsu|1=UNKNOWN}} (who fell during the {{ill|lt=Jōkyū War|Q1372308|qid=Q1372308|1=Jōkyū War}}), and the line beginning with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nishina Moritada|1=UNKNOWN}}, son of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Seki Morinaga|1=UNKNOWN}} and adopted son of Morikatsu.Shigeki Kitazawa, *The Nishina Clan*, Tokyo Tosho Shuppankai, 2010
Nishina Clan

=== The Great Cedar of Sometaruhonomikoto Shrine === A natural monument designated by Nagano City. With a height of about 30 meters and a trunk circumference at eye level of 4.82 meters, it is an exceptionally large {{ill|lt=Japanese cedar|Q147388|qid=Q147388|lt=cedar|1=Cryptomeria}} even among the shrine grove. Aside from holes made by {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=woodpeckers|1=UNKNOWN}}, there is no damage, the tree is vigorous, and its form is fine. It also stands in an appropriate location near the shrine buildings as a sacred tree. Although tall trees in this shrine grove have been struck by lightning several times in the past and damaged one by one, it is fortunate that this great cedar has never been struck by lightning to date.Partially quoted from Nagano City Board of Education, Cultural Properties Division, “[https://web.archive.org/web/20111221062200/https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5604.pdf#search='Sumetaruhonomikoto' The Great Cedar of Sometaruhonomikoto Shrine]”. === Notes === {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{ill|lt=Ken’ichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|qid=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}, ed., *Gods of Japan – Shrines and Sacred Places – 9: Mino, Hida, and Shinano*, {{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|qid=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}, 2000 === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Mount Iizuna|Q11666652|qid=Q11666652|1=Mount Iizuna}} * {{ill|lt=Nishina clan|Q11376452|qid=Q11376452|1=Nishina clan}} – produced shrine priests. === External links === * [http://www.iizuna-jinnjya.jp/ Iizuna Shrine Official Website] * [http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1091.html The Great Cedar of Sometaruhonomikoto Shrine] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Iizuna Shrine (Q17238950)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iizuna Shrine (Q17238950)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iizuna Shrine (Q17238950)]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]]
